pages.raw_text:
Every influence that can be
brought to bear on the members OF
the city council by opponents or
the initiative and referendum
amendment to the city charter is
being mustered to keep the coun-
Gil from placing the amendment be-
fore the people for their approval.
The liquor men without exception
fear the amendment because they
think it would give the residents
of any portion of the city the right
to refuse a license for an objection
able saloon.


The liquor men are not alone III
making the fight against the initia'
tive and referendum amendment
Under this amendment the fran
chises granted by the council would
have to be approved by the people,
as otherwise they could be voted
on and, if unpopular, defeated. The
corporations do not want to do
business with all the people. They
prefer to deal with few individ-
ual members of the council.


Mr.. Pratt and several other mem
bers of the council viii stand by
the proposed amendment, but ev-
cry man who opposes saloon and
corporation rule will have to take
an active interest in the proposed
amendment if it is to become a
law.


Dutch Jake" and others of his
stripe are working in every way
possible to defeat the amendment.
1t is up to the moral citizenship to
say which element controls the
city.
